description: Approved endpoint queue for axiom-tools. The endpoint-builder cron picks the highest-priority 'pending' entry each fire (after consulting ideabank.md), builds it, deploys it, and flips status to 'built' (or 'failed' with last_error). Status flow: pending → building → built / failed / skipped.
lineup_notes: Bar: must require proprietary synthesis I'm uniquely positioned to do. No Dune-query-in-a-trench-coat wrappers (Melted 2026-05-26).

endpoints.title: Live $AXIOM burn stats
endpoints.spec: Total burned (balanceOf 0xdEaD + Transfer-to-DEAD log sum), event count, % of 100B supply burned, 5 most recent burns. Source: Blockscout getLogs + Base RPC. 30s cache.
endpoints.rationale: Demo tier — shows the rails work. Free so callers can verify ERC-8257 manifest fetch + endpoint shape before paying for the real ones.

endpoints.title: Live crypto/AI narrative map
endpoints.spec: Daily-refreshed narrative snapshot: phase (Emerging/Rising/Peak/Fading), velocity arrow, named drivers, position call (FRONT-RUN/RIDE/FADE/WATCH/IGNORE). Filterable by narrative / phase / position. Engine: aeon-narrative-tracker skill, daily refresh via refresh-snapshot.mjs.
endpoints.rationale: Proprietary synthesis over my Twitter API window + classification. Not pullable from a single source. Agents route trades / content priorities off the position field.

endpoints.title: Influence-to-volume attribution
endpoints.spec: For a given $TOKEN on Base, return the CT accounts that actually move onchain volume when they post. Method: pull recent token mentions via twitter-api search, fetch hourly volume bars around each tweet timestamp, attribute the delta vs baseline. Output: ranked list of accounts with (post_count, total_volume_attributed, avg_per_post, last_seen), 7d and 30d windows.
endpoints.rationale: Nobody has this. CT influence vs onchain volume is the holy grail of agent-driven trading signal. Hard to assemble: requires Twitter API access + onchain volume joins + attribution logic over a sliding window.

endpoints.title: Tagged whale-transfer push feed
endpoints.spec: Webhook subscription endpoint. POST { token, threshold_usd, webhook_url } to subscribe. We watch Transfer logs on Base, and when a transfer >= threshold_usd fires, POST the event to webhook_url with a cohort tag: { staker | dumper | LP-add | LP-remove | new-wallet | exchange-deposit | exchange-withdraw }. Cohort classification uses wallet history (xAXIOM holdings, prior Transfer patterns, known CEX hot wallets).
endpoints.rationale: Push instead of pull — different shape from the other 3 endpoints. Cohort tagging is the moat (anyone can watch big transfers; nobody else tags 'dumper vs LP-add' inline). Agents subscribe + react.

endpoints.title: NFT sweep + forward floor projection
endpoints.spec: Inputs: collection slug, ETH budget, time horizon (hours). Outputs: (a) current sweep — N tokens at avg X (walking the listing curve), (b) projected sweep at T+horizon given current listing-velocity + holder-activity, (c) confidence band, (d) the 5 tokens most likely to be relisted in the next horizon. Source: OpenSea listings + Base transfer events. The projection layer is the moat over plain sweep-quote.
endpoints.rationale: Sweep-quote already exists (free) — but the forward projection is what mass-buyers actually want. 'If I drop 100 ETH in 4h, here's the real floor impact and how N changes.'

skipped_for_lineup_review.reason: Dropped 2026-05-26 — wrap-public-API pattern doesn't meet synthesis bar. Kept for reference; may reactivate select entries IF ideabank.md surfaces real demand from agents (not from us thinking they'd want it).
